title: Expandable Signal Navigator
prompt:
Create a production-ready Expandable Signal Navigator using React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. Make a compact navigation rail that can expand from icon-sized signal dots into labelled destinations: Overview, Signals, and Decisions. The active destination is represented by one coral signal line and readable text, not color alone. Use a pale #F4F5F6 field, white card, #ECECEE border, 22px radius, very soft shadow, #111111 text, and #4A4A4F secondary labels. Coral is the only bright accent; do not put gradients on the rail or card.

The disclosure button needs aria-expanded and aria-controls. Each destination is a semantic button with aria-current for the active item, a 44px minimum target, and a dark 3px visible focus ring. Clicking, Enter, or Space selects a destination; use a polite live region to announce it. Expand the rail over 220ms with opacity and width, move the coral signal line with a stiffness 320/damping 27 spring, lift a row 2px on hover over 180ms, and compress it 1px over 90ms. The collapsed state must still announce each destination through aria-labels.

At 320px, preserve one stable column; in a larger preview, allow the expanded labels without fixed positioning. Keep tab order predictable, use strong contrast, and avoid remote assets, storage, random values, network requests, timers, and dynamic code. For prefers-reduced-motion, reveal labels instantly or with a 120ms opacity transition. Return complete TypeScript React client code and an executable pure CSS approximation with suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* Suggested HTML:
Overview
*/ .uiwiki-signal-nav{width:208px;padding:12px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:22px;background:#fff}.uiwiki-signal-nav a,.uiwiki-signal-nav button{display:flex;align-items:center;min-height:44px;padding:0 12px;border:0;border-radius:12px;background:#fff;color:#111;font:700 14px system-ui}.uiwiki-signal-nav a[aria-current]{border-left:4px solid #fb7185}.uiwiki-signal-nav a:hover{transform:translateX(2px)}.uiwiki-signal-nav :focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.uiwiki-signal-nav *{transition:none!important}}
